title: Monopolios del Sector Construccion e Insumos — Argentina
author: Office of Accountability — automated research dossier
summary: Deep-dive into how Argentina's construction sector and its key inputs (cement, steel) are monopolized by a handful of families and foreign conglomerates. The public works cartel ('Club de la Obra Publica') operated for 40+ years through the Argentine Chamber of Road Companies ('La Camarita'), dividing contracts, rigging bids, and paying 10-20% kickbacks to officials. The 'Cuadernos' mega-trial (86 defendants, ongoing 2025-26) is the largest corruption case in Argentine history. Cement is an oligopoly of 4 firms (70%+ foreign-owned). Steel is a duopoly (Ternium/Techint + ArcelorMittal Acindar). Construction companies that dominate public works include Techint, IECSA/SACDE, Electroingenieria, Benito Roggio, Cartellone, and — during Kirchner era — Austral Construcciones (Lazaro Baez). Odebrecht paid USD 35M in bribes for Argentine projects. The system persisted across all administrations from the military dictatorship (1976) to present.

1_public_works_cartel.title: The Public Works Cartel — Club de la Obra Publica

mechanism.name: La Camarita (The Little Chamber)
mechanism.venue: Argentine Chamber of Road Companies (Camara Argentina de Empresas Viales — CAEV)
how_it_works: Before each bidding round, companies met at CAEV headquarters to pre-assign the winner
Losing bidders submitted inflated bids to make the winner's price appear competitive
Surplus from inflated prices covered: (a) 10-20% kickbacks to officials, (b) compensatory shares to 'losing' bidders
The Registro de Constructores (Registry of Constructors) under Law 13.064 legally restricted who could bid, creating a closed club
The 'capacidad constructiva' metric distributed work among existing members and blocked new entrants
Monthly distribution lists tracked: licitacion number, official budget, adjudicatee, offered amount, overprice percentage
mechanism.financial_circuit: Cash collected from companies by financial operators (e.g. Ernesto Clarens), converted to USD, delivered to officials. Clarens charged commission on exchange spread.
mechanism.total_estimated_graft: Over USD 30 million in road works alone (per fiscal accusation). 71 companies investigated.
mechanism.kickback_rate: 10-20% of contract value (per confession of Carlos Wagner, ex-president of Argentine Chamber of Construction)

sources.title: Fiscales.gob.ar — Cartelizacion de obra publica civil via CAEV
La Nacion — Cartelizacion: la genesis de la corrupcion en la obra publica
Jujuy al dia — Como la Camarita cartelizaba licitaciones

road_concessions_1988_alfonsin.note: First wave of road concessions under Alfonsin (1988)

reform_2025.description: Milei/Sturzenegger eliminated the Registro de Constructores and capacidad constructiva system (May 2025), replaced with SICO (Sistema de Informacion de Cocontratantes). Vialidad Nacional dissolved July 2025 (decree 461/2025).

2_cuadernos_case.title: Cuadernos de las Coimas — The Notebooks of Corruption
2_cuadernos_case.case_status: Oral trial began November 2025. Ongoing as of March 2026. Largest corruption case in Argentine history.
2_cuadernos_case.origin: Notebooks kept by Oscar Centeno, driver of Roberto Baratta (Secretary of Energy under Planning Minister Julio De Vido), documenting cash deliveries from companies to officials during Kirchner governments (2003-2015).

defendants.principal_defendant: Cristina Fernandez de Kirchner — accused of leading illicit association, 203 counts of passive bribery (cohecho pasivo). Potential sentence: 15+ years.
defendants.cristina_current_status: Under house arrest serving 6-year sentence for fraudulent administration in Causa Vialidad (confirmed by Supreme Court).

key_political_figures.name: Roberto Baratta
key_political_figures.role: Secretary of Energy, direct recipient of bribes

key_political_figures.name: Jose Lopez
key_political_figures.role: Planning Ministry official, received cash deliveries (caught throwing bags of USD 9M into a monastery in 2016)

key_political_figures.name: Ricardo Jaime
key_political_figures.role: Transport Secretary, received 5% kickbacks on subsidies

key_cooperating_witnesses.name: Carlos Enrique Wagner
key_cooperating_witnesses.role: Ex-president of Argentine Chamber of Construction. Confessed entire cartel mechanism.

key_cooperating_witnesses.name: Aldo Roggio
key_cooperating_witnesses.role: Head of Grupo Roggio. Admitted paying 5% of transport subsidies to officials. Offered USD 1,267,108 restitution.

key_cooperating_witnesses.name: Angelo Calcaterra
key_cooperating_witnesses.role: Former IECSA owner, Macri cousin. Admitted payments to Baratta for 2013/2015 Kirchner campaigns.

key_non_cooperating_defendants.name: Gerardo Ferreyra
key_non_cooperating_defendants.role: Electroingenieria. Imprisoned, maintains innocence. Supreme Court ruled against him Nov 2024.

key_non_cooperating_defendants.name: Paolo Rocca
key_non_cooperating_defendants.role: Techint. Implicated but executives obtained dismissal (sobreseimiento) in Cuadernos — ruling criticized as scandalous by legal analysts.

financial_operator.name: Ernesto Clarens
financial_operator.role: Financier. Received cash from companies, converted to USD, delivered to officials, charged commission.

2_cuadernos_case.total_road_works_value: ARS 3.3+ billion (Roggio alone received 22 works from Vialidad Nacional)
2_cuadernos_case.restitution_attempts: 48 defendants offered restitution payments (Sept 2025) to avoid worst sentencing outcomes.

sources.title: Infobae — Comienzan indagatorias juicio Cuadernos
Economis — Causa Cuadernos juicio noviembre 2025
SWI Swissinfo — Kirchner faces largest corruption trial
Fiscales.gob.ar — Cartelizacion de la obra publica

3_odebrecht_lava_jato.title: Odebrecht / Lava Jato — Argentine Branch
3_odebrecht_lava_jato.confession_date: 2016-12-21 (US District Court, Eastern District of New York — FCPA guilty plea)
3_odebrecht_lava_jato.convictions_in_argentina: ZERO as of 2026. Argentina is among the worst-performing countries in prosecuting Odebrecht bribes.

projects.name: Soterramiento del Ferrocarril Sarmiento
projects.bribe_mechanism: Fictitious 'technical assistance' contract with Spanish entity. USD 10.3M invoiced, USD 4.5M routed through Andorra for bribery.
projects.status: Work paralyzed, never completed. Macri government paid USD 759M (2016-2018). Investigation ongoing, no convictions.

projects.name: Planta Potabilizadora Parana de las Palmas (AySA)
projects.note: Per Chequeado investigation, constructoras sospechadas obtained contracts with USD 300M in overpricing.

local_partners.mechanism: Odebrecht testified: 'In Argentina, we made payments through local partners.' Argentine companies served as conduits for bribes.

implicated_officials.name: Gustavo Arribas
implicated_officials.role: Head of AFI (Federal Intelligence Agency) under Macri — indicted for receiving Odebrecht gifts

sources.title: La Nacion — Argentina sin condenas por Lava Jato
Quinto Elemento — Argentina sin condenas
Chequeado — Lava Jato en Argentina sobreprecios USD 300M
Infobae — Corte Suprema valida pruebas Lava Jato

4_austral_baez_kirchner.title: Austral Construcciones / Lazaro Baez — The Kirchner Construction Empire

origin_story.career: Bank employee at Banco Nacion and Banco Provincia de Santa Cruz. Rose to branch manager (1991) under Kirchner's governorship.
origin_story.relationship_to_kirchner: Met Nestor Kirchner in 1990, one year before Kirchner became governor of Santa Cruz.
origin_story.enrichment: From bank clerk to construction magnate. Patrimony grew 2,200% in 5 years. By 2017, fortune valued at ARS 2.75 billion.
origin_story.testaferro_allegations: Repeatedly accused of being Kirchner's frontman (testaferro). Federico Elaskar and Leonardo Farina testified to money laundering operations channeling EUR 55 million to Swiss banks in 6 months.

company.name: Austral Construcciones S.A.
company.contracts: 51 public works contracts in Santa Cruz province (2003-2015)
company.completion_rate: 50% of 51 works remained incomplete as of December 2015 (per Vialidad Nacional report)
company.monopoly_in_santa_cruz: Near-total monopoly of road construction in Santa Cruz province through Austral and satellite companies

judicial_outcomes.causa_vialidad: Supreme Court confirmed 6-year prison sentence and perpetual disqualification from office for Cristina Kirchner for directing public works in favor of Baez.
judicial_outcomes.baez_money_laundering: Baez convicted of money laundering. Under investigation for additional charges.
judicial_outcomes.asset_seizure_2025: Government of Santa Cruz took possession of Austral's main facilities (November 2025). Assets being auctioned.

sources.title: Wikipedia — Caso Grupo Austral
La Nacion — Como estan las rutas de Lazaro Baez
Perfil — Quebro Austral Construcciones
La Nacion — Lazaro Baez empleado bancario a multimillonario
Cronista — De empleado bancario a rey de la obra publica

5_cement_monopoly.title: Cement Oligopoly — 4 Firms, 96% Foreign-Controlled
5_cement_monopoly.market_structure: Highly concentrated. Competition authority (CNDC) found 'high degree of concentration, high barriers to entry, and reduced competitive dynamics.'

market_shares.controller: InterCement (Camargo Correa, Brazil) — sale pending to Latcem (Marcelo Mindlin) + Redwood Capital + Moneda-Patria

competition_concerns.cndc_investigation: National Competition Defense Commission published market investigation finding 'localized market power' through price discrimination, generating significant overcharging for construction.
competition_concerns.information_exchange: CNDC flagged information exchange between the 4 companies via the Portland Cement Manufacturers Association (AFCP). Companies were forced to modify practices.
competition_concerns.barriers_to_entry: High capital requirements, geographic distribution of plants, import logistics make new entry extremely difficult.

loma_negra_history.iconic_owner: Amalia Lacroze de Fortabat (widow, managed company for decades)
loma_negra_history.camargo_correa_corruption: Camargo Correa implicated in Brazilian Lava Jato investigation. InterCement entered judicial restructuring.
loma_negra_history.pending_sale_2025: Creditors approved restructuring plan (99% support). New owners: Marcelo Mindlin (Latcem), Redwood Capital, Moneda-Patria. Execution deadline: 2028.
loma_negra_history.mindlin_note: Same Marcelo Mindlin who bought IECSA (now SACDE). If sale completes, Mindlin will control largest cement producer AND a major construction company.

5_cement_monopoly.holcim_expansion: Swiss group entered Argentina acquiring Minetti (1998), then Corcemar. Expanding beyond cement into construction materials and services.

sources.title: Cohen Perspectivas — Oportunidades en cementeras
Ambito — Concentracion de mercado cementeras
Semanario de Junin — Cartelizacion precios altos cemento
Ambito — Loma Negra venta InterCement reestructuracion
Infobae — Se vende Loma Negra Camargo Correa

6_steel_monopoly.title: Steel Duopoly — Ternium/Techint + ArcelorMittal Acindar
6_steel_monopoly.market_structure: Near-monopoly. 'Not a perfect monopoly, but almost' (industry analyst). Two groups control virtually all domestic production.

players.history: In December 1993, flat steel production in Argentina became concentrated/monopolized in Siderar (later Ternium).
players.recent_acquisitions: Acquired major Argentine tube manufacturer to consolidate control against Chinese competition (2025).
players.milei_era: Government opened market to imports. Sturzenegger accused Techint of charging 40% more than Indian competitors for pipes. Ternium alarmed by Chinese steel entering under unfair trade conditions.

players.product: Long steel (for construction — rebar, wire, profiles)
players.note: Production at 20-year low in 2024 due to economic crisis. Villa Constitucion plant halved output.
players.history: Acindar founded 1942, acquired by ArcelorMittal (Lakshmi Mittal) in 2000s.

import_protection_history.traditional: Argentina historically shielded domestic steel producers with tariffs, anti-dumping duties, and non-tariff barriers.
import_protection_history.milei_deregulation: Current government advancing deregulatory plan allowing foreign steel entry. Forces Techint to compete for first time in decades.
import_protection_history.chinese_threat: Chinese steel tubes entering Argentine market at much lower prices, especially for Vaca Muerta energy infrastructure projects.

transfer_pricing.mechanism: Techint group routes profits through Uruguayan shell companies with no productive activity. Intangible assets (brands, patents developed by Argentine researchers) transferred to offshore structures.
transfer_pricing.profit_margin_shift: 15-22% of gross profits explained by transfer pricing via Uruguay.

sources.title: Ambito — Ternium revirtio perdidas operativas
Reportacero — Techint vs Chinese steel
iProfesional — Planes de Ternium
Pagina 12 — Controlando a los monopolios
Camara Argentina del Acero — Ternium compra fabricante de tubos

families.name: Rocca (Techint Group)
families.patriarch: Agostino Rocca (Italian, ex-advisor to Mussolini on steel, founded Techint 1945)
families.current_head: Paolo Rocca (CEO, Forbes 2026 #528 global, richest Argentine, USD 7.3B net worth)
families.empire: USD 22 billion revenue, 52,000 employees. Controls Tenaris, Ternium, Tecpetrol, SIAT, Techint Ingenieria.
families.first_major_contract: 33,000 tons seamless pipes for Gas del Estado (1947). 1,600 km gas pipeline under Peron (1949).

judicial_status.italy: Prosecution requested 4.5 years for international corruption (bribes to Petrobras). Acquitted on jurisdictional grounds (Milan court declared lack of jurisdiction, May 2022).
judicial_status.argentina_cuadernos: Techint executives obtained sobreseimiento (dismissal). Ruling widely criticized. Appeal filed by prosecutors.
judicial_status.brazil_lava_jato: Techint subsidiary Confab paid USD 10.4M to Petrobras executive Renato De Sousa Duque (convicted).

sources.title: elDiarioAR — Los Rocca presuntos sobornos contratos
Perfil — Piden 4 anos para Paolo Rocca
Infobae — Paolo Rocca absuelto en Italia

families.name: Macri (SOCMA / IECSA)
families.patriarch: Franco Macri (Italian immigrant, built SOCMA holding: Fiat Argentina, Canale, Correo Argentino, IECSA, Sideco)
families.political_figure: Mauricio Macri (Franco's son, President of Argentina 2015-2019)
iecsa_history: Transferred to Angelo Calcaterra (Mauricio's cousin) in 2007 for USD 61M when Macri became Buenos Aires Mayor
Under Calcaterra, formed alliances with Odebrecht and Ghella for mega-projects
Calcaterra sold to Marcelo Mindlin (2017) when Macri became President — renamed SACDE
Evidence suggests Calcaterra retained hidden control through Delaware offshore entity shared with Mindlin
families.calcaterra_judicial_status: Declared arrepentido in Cuadernos (admitted payments to Baratta). Contradicted himself in Odebrecht case (denied bribes). Casacion removed him from Cuadernos trial (June 2024, challenged).

sources.title: La Nacion — IECSA negocios bajo sospecha
Wikipedia — IECSA
Tiempo Argentino — Calcaterra sigue detras de IECSA
El Destape — Calcaterra sigue siendo dueno de IECSA

families.name: Ferreyra (Electroingenieria)
families.current_head: Gerardo Ferreyra (co-founder, ex-VP, ex-member of ERP — People's Revolutionary Army urban guerrilla)
families.empire: Energy infrastructure, power plants, dams, nuclear. USD 1 billion in revenue from Cordoba public works alone over 17 years.

key_irregularity.note: Sole bidder. Original 2007 bid was for different project (repowering old plant), abandoned, then awarded to Electroingenieria-Sener UTE in 2008 without new public call.

families.judicial_status: Did NOT become arrepentido. Imprisoned Aug 2018, later granted house arrest. Supreme Court ruled against him Nov 2024. Maintains case was 'fabricated to destroy national businessmen.'

sources.title: Perfil — Gerardo Ferreyra arrepentidos relato falso
iProfesional — Electroingenieria facturo USD 1000M en Cordoba
La Nacion — Corte Suprema fallo contra Ferreyra

families.name: Roggio (Grupo Roggio)
families.founding: 1908. Grew dramatically under military dictatorship (1976-83) — built ATC, Chateau Carreras stadium for 1978 World Cup.
families.cuadernos_status: Arrepentido. Admitted paying 5% of transport subsidies to Secretary Ricardo Jaime. 11 documented bribery payments (June 2009 - Sept 2010) totaling ARS 5.9M. Received 22 works from Vialidad Nacional worth ARS 3.3B+.
families.offshore: Used 3 foreign companies to open accounts in Switzerland and USA (Pandora Papers).

sources.title: La Izquierda Diario — Grupo Roggio dictadores y corrupcion
Infobae — Roggio arrepentido Pandora Papers
Infobae — Roggio capitan de industria que regateo coimas

families.name: Fortabat / Camargo Correa (Loma Negra)
families.history: Founded 1926 by Alfredo Fortabat. Run by widow Amalia Lacroze de Fortabat for decades. Sold to Camargo Correa (Brazil) in 2005 for USD 1B.
families.current_transition: InterCement (Camargo subsidiary) in judicial restructuring. New owners pending: Marcelo Mindlin (Latcem), Redwood Capital, Moneda-Patria. Deadline 2028.
families.monopoly_note: Controls 45.6% of Argentine cement market. If Mindlin acquisition completes, same individual will control Loma Negra + SACDE (construction) — unprecedented vertical integration.

8_bidding_rigging.title: How Bids Are Rigged — Mechanisms and Evidence

mechanisms.description: Companies met at CAEV to receive monthly lists assigning which company would win each upcoming licitacion. Lists included: licitacion number, official budget, designated winner, bid amount, overprice percentage.
mechanisms.evidence: Confessions of Carlos Wagner and multiple arrepentidos. Physical lists recovered in investigation.

mechanisms.description: Technical specifications written to match only one company's capabilities, ensuring a sole bidder.
examples: SIAT (Techint) sole bidder for Gasoducto NK pipe supply (USD 500M)
Electroingenieria sole bidder for EPEC Pilar power plant

mechanisms.description: The 'capacidad constructiva' metric under the Registro de Constructores legally limited bidding to established cartel members. New companies could not accumulate enough 'capacity points' to qualify for major works.
Mega-projects packaged to require consortium bids (UTEs — Uniones Transitorias de Empresas), ensuring multiple cartel members share each project.
examples: Odebrecht + IECSA + Ghella + Comsa for Sarmiento burial
Odebrecht + Supercemento + Roggio + Cartellone for AySA plant

mechanisms.description: Cartel companies received contracts under every government: military dictatorship, Alfonsin, Menem, De la Rua, Kirchner, Macri. The same companies appear in the 1981 complaint and the 2025 trial.
mechanisms.evidence: Comparison of 1981 cartel complaint defendants with 2025 Cuadernos defendants shows identical family names: Macri, Rocca, Roggio.

revolving_door.statistic: 24% of Argentine government officials (86/364 studied) came directly from private sector executive positions.
revolving_door.cooling_off_period: Argentina's Ethics Law (25188) originally required 1-year cooling-off. Modified in 2001 to remove this requirement entirely.

sources.title: SCIOTECA/CAF — Inside the Revolving Door: Campaign Finance, Lobbying, Meetings and Public Contracts in Argentina
UNSAM — Puerta Giratoria en Argentina

9_political_connections.title: Contractor-Politician Nexus and Campaign Finance
money_loop: 1. Companies receive state contracts (public works, concessions, subsidies)
3. Cash from margin collected by financial operators (Clarens et al.)
4. Cash converted to USD, delivered to officials (documented in Centeno notebooks)
5. Some funds go to electoral campaign financing (Calcaterra admitted funding 2013/2015 campaigns)
6. Companies legally prohibited from donating to campaigns do so via undeclared 'black money'
7. Officials who receive funds ensure continued contract flow to same companies
8. Registry of Constructors (until 2025) legally blocked new competitors from entering

campaign_finance_reality.law: 2019 reform enabled corporate donations, but companies with public works contracts remain prohibited from donating.
campaign_finance_reality.practice: Per Poder Ciudadano (Transparency International chapter): most campaign money remains 'black' (undeclared).
campaign_finance_reality.transport_subsidy_variant: Roggio admitted returning 5% of transport subsidies (Metrovias/Ferrovias) directly to Transport Secretary Ricardo Jaime.

key_contractor_politician_relationships.nature: Alleged testaferro. Baez controlled 51 contracts in Kirchners' home province.
Family company. 'Sold' when Macri entered politics, but ownership obfuscated through offshore.
Consistent major contractor across all administrations. 'Every government with its corresponding Rocca pen' (reference to officials who sign contracts).

key_contractor_politician_relationships.politician: Kirchner governments + Jose Manuel De la Sota (Cordoba governor)
key_contractor_politician_relationships.nature: Favored firm of Kirchner era in energy sector and Cordoba provincial works.
Grew under Videla (1976). Received subway concession under Menem (1994). Admitted bribery under Kirchner.
Paid bribes under Kirchner. Macri's AFI chief (Arribas) also indicted for Odebrecht gifts.

ppp_consorcios_2018.description: First PPP (Public-Private Partnership) round for 3,300 km of highways. 10 consortia, 19 national + 7 international firms, 32 bids.
ppp_consorcios_2018.note: Many of the same cartel members appear in PPP consortia. Panedile owners declared as arrepentidos in Cuadernos.

10_neo4j_crossref.title: Cross-Reference with Existing Neo4j Graph Data
10_neo4j_crossref.note: The existing monopolios-crossref-neo4j.json contains 240 findings from graph database. Top contractors by number of PublicContract nodes are dominated by telecom and utilities, not construction — indicating the current graph data captures procurement/licensing contracts rather than public works construction contracts.

top_contractors_in_graph.name: TELECOM ARGENTINA STET FRANCE TELECOM S.A.

10_neo4j_crossref.gap_analysis: Construction companies (Techint, Roggio, IECSA/SACDE, Electroingenieria, Cartellone, Austral) are likely underrepresented or absent in the current graph. A future ingestion wave should target Vialidad Nacional contract data (now available via SICO/CONTRATAR portal) and the 71 companies listed in the Cuadernos case.

vertical_integration_risk.title: Mindlin Convergence — Emerging Vertical Integration
vertical_integration_risk.description: Marcelo Mindlin is positioned to control a vertically integrated construction empire: SACDE (construction, ex-IECSA) + Loma Negra (45.6% of cement market) + Pampa Energia (energy). If the InterCement acquisition completes by 2028, one individual will control the largest construction company AND the largest cement supplier in Argentina.
vertical_integration_risk.concern: A single economic group controlling both the demand side (construction company that buys cement) and supply side (cement producer) creates potential for self-dealing, transfer pricing manipulation, and exclusion of competitors.

timeline.event: Loma Negra founded by Alfredo Fortabat
Techint wins first major contract: 33,000 tons pipes for Gas del Estado under Peron
Military dictatorship begins. Roggio group favored with World Cup construction.
First documented cartel complaint filed — names Techint, Macri, SADE, Perez Companc
First road concessions under Alfonsin: Roggio 22%, SOCMA/Macri 17%, Techint 11%
Steel production monopolized: Siderar (Techint) for flat, Acindar for long products
Roggio wins Buenos Aires subway concession under Menem
Kirchner presidency begins. Austral Construcciones (Baez) founded same year. Electroingenieria rises.
Camargo Correa (Brazil) buys Loma Negra for USD 1B
IECSA transferred from Macri family to cousin Calcaterra. Sarmiento burial awarded to Odebrecht-IECSA consortium.
Odebrecht guilty plea in New York — admits USD 35M in Argentine bribes. Jose Lopez caught with USD 9M in monastery.
Austral Construcciones declared bankrupt. IECSA sold to Mindlin, renamed SACDE.
Cuadernos case erupts. Mass arrests of businessmen and officials. Multiple arrepentidos. PPP highway bids.
Paolo Rocca acquitted in Italy on jurisdictional grounds.
CLISA (Roggio) enters default. Casacion removes Calcaterra from Cuadernos (challenged). Milei freezes public works.

Causa Vialidad: Supreme Court confirms Cristina Kirchner 6-year sentence. Cuadernos oral trial begins Nov 2025. Registro de Constructores eliminated. Vialidad Nacional dissolved. Austral assets seized.
Cuadernos trial ongoing — 86 defendants. Cristina Kirchner under house arrest. Indagatorias began March 2026.

metadata.data_quality: High confidence on cartel structure, judicial proceedings, and market shares (sourced from fiscal accusations, judicial records, competition authority reports, Forbes). Medium confidence on exact bribe amounts (based on confessions and Odebrecht plea deal). Low confidence on current offshore structures (limited public disclosure).
related_research_files: construction-cartel.json — detailed company profiles and cartel mechanism
recommended_next_steps: Ingest Vialidad Nacional/CONTRATAR open data for PublicWorksContract nodes
Create Construction entity type in Neo4j with CUIT-based resolution against existing Contractor nodes
Map UTE (consortium) relationships as edges between Construction companies
Link Cuadernos defendants to existing Person nodes via DNI cross-reference
Track Mindlin vertical integration: SACDE + Loma Negra + Pampa Energia edges
